data.title: PayPal's Growth And Buybacks Are Expected To Double Shareholders Returns
data.description:
Investment Thesis

PayPal Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:PYPL) presents a highly attractive investment opportunity. Its current P/E ratio of slightly under 15 underscores how the market continues to undervalue its long-term cash flow potential. Additionally, growth in net income, expansion of the PayPal world to facilitate global trade, and strategic partnerships with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and TerraPay are strengthening its ecosystem and total addressable market.

PayPal's $7 billion sale of its U.S. Buy Now Pay Later [BNPL] receivables to Blue Owl Capital is another major catalyst marking its transition to a “light balance sheet.” It mitigates credit risk while preserving customer relationships. With a strong free cash flow exceeding $5 billion annually and current 2025 TTM ROIC of 21.8%, ROE of 23%, and ROA of 5.7%, PayPal demonstrates efficient capital deployment.

The company is trading at $67 per share, while its fair value based on its free cash flow stands at $143 per share at a discount rate of 8%, offering more than 100% upside supported by multiple catalysts driving its revaluation and price. PayPal’s $15 billion share repurchase authorization, following a $6 billion buyback in 2024, indicates a significant reduction in outstanding shares by over 2 billion in the next few years could increase its earnings per share and double shareholder value.

Company Overview

PayPal operates as a global two-sided payments platform. It helps connect consumers and merchants across 200 markets with over 434 million active accounts.

PayPal generates its revenues mainly through transaction fees, crypto services, currency conversion, instant transfer, value added offerings such as credit products, partnerships, and subscriptions.

Business Model

PayPal operates a platform-based, transaction-driven business model strengthened by convenient digital transactions. It provides digital wallets through PayPal and Venmo, supporting payment through bank accounts, reward cards, balances, and cryptocurrencies. In addition, it also provides peer-to-peer transfers, buy now pay later, and access to credit products. For merchants, it delivers online and in-person payment solutions such as PayPal checkout, payout services, Zettle and Braintree. Merchants also benefit from financing options, global customer reach, and management tools.

The company generates revenue through transaction fees, which represent about 91% of its total revenue. Other services like value-added services, including consumer and merchant credit products, partnerships, referral fees, and interest on customer balances, also contribute to its revenue.

PayPal’s cost structure is driven by payment processing, regulatory requirements, and compliance, as well as ongoing investments in technology, sales, and marketing.

Business Segments

PayPal reports its operations under two main segments:

Transaction revenue: Revenue generated from fees charged to consumers and merchants for payment transactions across PayPal, Braintree, Venmo, Zettie, PayPal Pay Later, PayPal Complete Payments, and cryptocurrency services. This segment generated $28.84 billion in 2024 and $7.4 billion in Q2 2025, indicating 89.8% of net revenue with a 4% y-o-y increase.

Value-added services: This segment includes revenue from referral fees, gateway services, partnerships, interest, and fees on consumer as well as merchant credit products like PayPal Business and PayPal Working Capital, and Buy Now Pay Later. In addition to this, the revenue is also generated through interest income earned on customer balances. Revenue generated from this segment in 2024 was $2.95 billion and $847 million in Q2 2025.

PayPal’s Strength

1. Positive And Growing Free Cash Flows

PayPal has consistently generated positive free cash flows, growing more than 10% over the past 3 years due to its unique business model that requires lower capital to sustain its business and generate higher margins. PayPal is highly trusted, and its recognized payment option is what drives customers as well as merchants' confidence.

2. Share Buybacks

PayPal has been repurchasing shares for the last 5 years, leveraging its strong free cash flows to return excess capital to its shareholders. PayPal has spent more than $30 billion since 2015 to buy back shares.

In 2024, the company repurchased 92 million shares, spending approximately $6 billion as part of the company’s surplus capital return strategy. The goal was to enhance shareholder value as well as reduce the number of shares outstanding. The board authorized an additional $15 billion stock repurchase program with no expiration date in 2024.

It reduced approximately 16% of its outstanding shares from 1.10 billion in 2023 to approximately 977 million in 2025, thus creating significant shareholder value.

3. Platform Scale And Strategic Partnership With Google

With 434 million active accounts, PayPal can leverage its transactional data to personalize experiences by identifying the trends and unlocking new opportunities to generate more revenue. Its strategic partnership with Google is to enhance e-commerce and payment. Therefore, its collaboration with Google and other tech companies, merchants, and payment networks is to expand the company’s reach and reinforce its role around the global payment ecosystem.

PayPal also committed to investing $100 million to accelerate digital payment solutions for businesses as well as partnering with TerraPay to enable real-time fund transfer across Africa and the Middle East.

Weaknesses

1. Overreliance On Branded Checkouts, Growing Debt, And Cost Cuts

The company’s checkout button is its core profit driver, which generates nearly 80% of its profits. Big players like Apple Pay, as well as Google Pay, are growing on mobile, and merchants are selecting cheaper options. Since profits are so concentrated in checkouts, a small market share loss can significantly hit its earnings.

In addition, historically the company has been maintaining a low debt level, relying on its strong free cash flows to fund its operations. However, in recent years, the company’s debt has been increasing, which would reduce its free cash flows if interest rates remain high.

Also in January 2024, the company announced it would cut 2,500 jobs, which were spread across departments, targeting overlapping functions and cutting down on less profitable initiatives. This decision was part of the company’s strategic effort to “right size,” and the goal was to streamline operations, reduce duplication, complexity to enhance efficiency.

Financial Analysis

Growing Revenue & Net Income (2025 TTM)

In the last 3 years, the company’s revenue grew from $29.77 billion in 2023 to $32.29 billion in 2025 [TTM] indicating an increase of 8%. This is a result of the company successfully expanding its user base and monetizing it. However, PayPal now is facing the challenge of maintaining and sustaining this growth due to stiff competition from other platforms like Stripe and Wise plc (OTCPK:WPLCF).

Despite these challenges, PayPal’s net income has indicated a strong upward trajectory, which nearly doubled since 2022, increasing to $4.67 billion in 2025 [TTM] at a CAGR of 17.93%. However, its net income has been more volatile, despite growing year over year on average with strong margin expansion close to 14%.

Since 2023, the company, despite earlier losses, managed to achieve $383 million in net gains on its strategic investments and interest income.

In the last 3 years, its performance improved mainly due to growth in revenue, operating efficiently, and improving the performance of its strategic investments that boosted its net income. Moreover, PayPal’s increasing debt to $11.30 billion in 2025 from $9.6 billion has negatively affected its free cash flow. Its FCF to debt dropped below 50% in 2025.

PayPal FCF has decreased from $6.76 billion in 2024 to $5.29 billion in 2025 [TTM].

FCF-Based Valuation

Assumptions Reason
Revenue Growth Expected to grow at 12% vs 14% historical avg. growth in the next 5 years
FCF Growth Expected to grow at 10% vs historical avg. growth of 15% in the next 5 years
Earnings Growth Expected to grow at 14% based on its historical avg. growth in the next 5 years
Discount Rate (Opportunity Cost) 8% based on the Fed's rate cut in the last meeting
Outstanding Shares (Diluted) 1.039M (2024). Diluted outstanding shares reduced to 977M in 2025 [TTM]
Perpetuity Growth Rate 3% (expected rate of inflation in the medium to long term)
Period 5 Yrs. Medium term

Estimated Fair Value

Based on PayPal’s FCF growth for the last 5 years, I expect the sum of its free cash flows to grow to $27 billion after discounting at 8%. Therefore, the company seems to be undervalued at its current market price of $66.89. Its fair value should be close to $143 per share based on my FCF based intrinsic value calculation. As support for my thesis, the company is already making significant changes, such as ongoing product diversification by offering expanded crypto features, which is expected to boost its revenues. PayPal World has strengthened its core business for profitability, which underpins the management’s efforts to maximize long-term value for its shareholders.

Catalysts Driving PayPal's Value

PayPal’s performance has been strong in Q2 2025, which signals an operational efficiency and potential for the stock to grow close to its fair value in the near future. The company’s Q2 report highlights improved profitability, supported by an expanding total addressable market, strategic product innovation, and share repurchases.

Based on PayPal’s share buyback program, approximately 2.14 billion shares are expected to be reduced over the next 3-4 years. The reduction in outstanding shares acts as a major catalyst for uplifting the company’s stock price, let alone revenue and FCF growth, which are also expected to grow above 11% based on its last 3 years trend.

Therefore, based on its current run rate for its share buybacks, PayPal’s current stock price could potentially multiply by nearly 7 times, creating shareholder value.

PayPal had a strong momentum for its Venmo platform, where double-digit revenue growth continues as the company expands its checkout points, merchant partnerships, card usage. Its buy now and pay later volumes also accelerated, and active account trends also significantly improved, with the company beginning to re-engage with dormant users, thus creating an opportunity to deepen cross-selling across its ecosystem.

PayPal announced the launch of PayPal World Platform that aims to facilitate global trade. Through this platform, the customers will be able to use their local wallets and payment methods to make payments to each other. PayPal now is investing heavily in artificial intelligence to improve streamlining checkout processes and to improve fraud detection and also to personalize its user experience.

PayPal’s $7 Billion Sale Of Its U.S. Buy Now Pay Later Receivables To Blue Owl Capital Acts As A Catalyst

This deal allows the company to transfer credit risk off its balance sheet while continuing to handle underwriting and servicing, meaning PayPal can still keep control over its customer relationships and the Buy Now Pay Later portfolio. This move indicates the company’s shift towards a “light balance sheet model,” reducing its exposure to potential loan defaults and improving efficiency, which was affecting its profitability.

Other Key Valuation Metrics Supporting PayPal’s Fair Value

PayPal key valuation metrics indicate strong underlying profitability and operational efficiency that support our thesis that PayPal’s current market value will move towards its fair value in the short to medium term. The company’s ROIC of 21.8% in 2025 [TTM] indicates a highly efficient capital allocation strategy by the company’s management using capital effectively.

In addition, its current ROE of 23% and ROA of 5.7% this year in 2025 (TTM) suggest above-average returns relative to its competitors from the financial industry. These returns highlight PayPal’s effective management policy of consistently generating above-average returns by keeping its assets and equity levels lower to mitigate risk and return excess capital to its rightful owners, i.e., its shareholders, through share buybacks.

PayPal’s current P/E ratio of under 15 remains well below the market and peer valuation, indicating PayPal is deeply undervalued.

Key Challenges And Risks

PayPal faces high competition from fintech giants like Apple (AAPL), Google Pay, and Stripe and other emerging players in Buy Now Pay Later companies like Affirm. Earlier in 2001, the company had problems with online fraud as a result of international hackers targeting the company’s accounts and transferring small amounts of money out of multiple accounts. But the company has since then developed a monitoring system to detect fraudulent transactions.

However, despite these efforts, in 2015 around 150,000 Spanish cardholders faced their funds being frozen by regulators, apparently affecting PayPal customers.

PayPal Penalties And Regulatory Settlements

Date

Regulatory Body

Violation Description

Penalty Amount

2020

OFAC U.S. Treasury

Transactions (2009-2013) involving sanctioned entities in Cuba, Iran, Sudan, and WMD proliferators due to inadequate screening filters

$7.7 million Settlement

2025

NYDFS

Cybersecurity failure exposing approximately 35,000 users’ SSNs via unsecured IRS Form 1099-K access.

$2 million fine

The company has been regularly hit by fines for breach of sanctions to carry out transactions involving sanctioned countries and entities in Iran, Sudan, Cuba, and other groups linked to weapons of mass destruction. PayPal’s screening filters were inadequate, and its compliance systems failed to block such transactions. And the company had to pay $7.7 million as a settlement fee for such violations in 2020.

There was another cybersecurity lapse involving exposure to the social security number of about 35,000 users. This breach occurred due to unsecured access to IRD form 1099-K data. The company had to pay a $2 million fine as recently as 2025.

Therefore, despite investing in technology and creating detective tools, PayPal still runs the risk of data breaches. In addition, changes in data privacy laws and financial regulations can negatively impact the company’s operations unless it has stronger controls.

Technical glitches can disrupt services, which can lead to user dissatisfaction and loss of revenue. PayPal also faces several market-related risks and is exposed to interest rate risk due to current changes in interest rates being lowered, affecting its income earned on its short-term investments such as Treasuries and bond portfolios. Adverse movement in interest rates reduces customer spending and also increases delinquencies on PayPal's credit products.

Besides this, currency markets have also been very volatile recently due to trade wars between the US, China, and the EU, and exchange rate movements have also put pressure on its money transfer loss fee.

PayPal is heavily dependent on its revenue from foreign exchange rates due to its large international operations in the UK, Europe, and Australia. Therefore, adverse movement in exchange rates for these countries has a huge impact on PayPal’s earnings and its cash flows.

In order to mitigate these risks, the company uses an effective hedging strategy to deal with volatility in exchange rates, but it could affect its financials to some extent, which in turn could possibly incur minor losses for its business.

The company also made strategic investments close to $1.6 billion in 2025 in some public and private companies and incurred a significant loss, which has affected its profitability and may continue for a while unless it decides to exit some of these non-profitable minority positions.

Conclusion

PayPal offers an attractive investment opportunity for its shareholders and investors. Despite significant operational risks and regulatory breaches and fines, which have resulted in its share price losing value, the company has the potential to recover and reach its estimated fair value of $143 in the short to medium term. Its free cash flow strength, omnichannel presence, and ability to innovate across payments and financial services provide a solid foundation for long-term growth despite all the challenges and stiff competition in the payment industry. While risks from increasing levels of its debt remain a genuine concern, it still has the ability to scale due to its brand trust and generate and grow its FCF.

Moreover, the historical performance suggests that it consistently produces profits and can withstand any external pressure. Therefore, it currently trades at a significant discount to its fair value, and the company presents a compelling risk-reward opportunity to investors seeking exposure to the digital payment sector who wish to invest medium to long-term to double their investment in the next 3-5 years.

data.title: ASML Results Update: Price Power Remains Key In A Growing Market
data.description:
At current market valuation levels, the earnings season will add crucial information for the continuation of the recent bull market. ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) is traditionally among the first firms to report, together with some banks, and may set the tone for the expected continuation of positive results related to companies operating in the AI environment. This happens in an environment where rich valuations and historical highs are pushed by general margin benefits in several sectors in the US market, potentially pushed by productivity gains related to AI. Nevertheless, both in terms of margins and valuations, the AI hype has the potential to scare investors, even considering the solid sales and margins increases, reflecting benefits on cash conversion for a consistent number of players. A one-year P/E evaluation with the most important players in the AI hardware landscape reveals some sort of stability, and in some cases moderation, in the ratio, despite the stock performance as a result of consistent earnings growth. The positiveness around ASML in the premarket on the Oct/15th is, in this article, to be analyzed as a trade reaction or a fundamental based movement.

Investment Thesis

In a previous article, I mentioned the scientific basis of Intel. ASML is another firm that can trace its history to this edge-of-knowledge development from its foundation to current days. Founded in 1984 in the Netherlands, ASML succeeded in a competitive market, facing both American and Japanese contenders, to finally establish itself as a sort of monopoly in the top-tier lithography market. The equipment, initially conceived by Jay Lathrop which decided to invert the lenses of his microscope to illuminate photo-sensible chemicals on a chipset and therefore print integrated circuits, played a critical role in allowing for continuous size reduction and circuits multiplication in one chipset.

Nowadays, the market can be divided in two main products. The Deep Ultraviolet (DUV) products are standard equipment for chip production. They allow for mass production of chipsets that are not in the forefront of capacity, as the technology and the light wavelength used produce a larger size circuit. At this point, our society is moving to a chip-everywhere situation, meaning that the production and demand for general purpose chipset remains strong, pushing for expansion of production and lithography.

The most developed market, on the other hand, utilizes Ultra-Deep Ultraviolet (EUV), which is capable to print integrated circuits that are moving towards 3nm (nanometer). Yes! One nanometer is equal to one billionth of a meter – for the sake of comparison, a human hair has 80K – 100K nm wide. EVU’s expected CAGR is 11.8% to 2034. As one can imagine, such a small circuit allow for the agglomeration of almost countless number of transistors into one chipset, opening the field for computing capacity increases that is unthinkable using previous technologies. This is the exact point where the growing computing demand of AI is only achievable as these advanced machines are utilized in chip production.

If the demand for AI models and applications is growing and expected to remain elevated in the foreseeable future, the same applies to the hardware powering these solutions, more specifically the GPUs. As these very high capacity and efficiency GPUs are only viable through the utilization of advanced lithography equipment, it is possible to infer that the demand is healthy. Moreover, the fact that ASML reigns in this environment, it is also possible to infer that the company possesses a relatively strong pricing power in these products, even despite a certain concentration in the customers. All these conclusions are fair, but any evaluation of the lithography thesis can only be done after some analysis on the geopolitical aspect of the semiconductor market.

The many sovereign AI efforts around the globe produce a duplication effect on the investment. The company identified around US$ 200 million in direct government support in terms of tax credits and direct investment in the US, Europe, India, China, South Korea, Taiwan and Japan. This effect may be magnified if considered that supply chain internalization is under process in many nations, resulting in 5 – 8% extra foundry capacity on top of ‘normal’ expansion driven by demand.

In the first half of 2024, China represented 41.6% of total turnover. During this year, an escalation in trade tensions, especially in the tech sector, took place. The updated version of Advanced Computing and Semiconductor Manufacturing Rule resulted in export restrictions on EUV and certain DUV equipment. In October, further US export controls on chips impose additional impositions for the lithography supplier. From a Chinese perspective, retaliations related to rare earths and minerals have the potential to disrupt ASML’s supply chain. Moreover, Pekin also recently introduced import restrictions on semiconductors and related equipment, trying to foster domestic development and independence. As a result, the participation of China in the top line reduced to 24%, with both Taiwan and the US increasing purchases from ASML in the first half of the year. In Q3, nevertheless, deliveries to China increased back to 42%. The uncertainty around the China trade brings two important implications to ASML. First, quarterly results may present abnormal volatility, depending on unpredictable escalation and de-escalation. As a result, a financial burden is imposed to the firm. Secondly, despite still not relevant, China may want to develop own solutions and foster a future competitor to ASML, more or less in the same pattern as happening with Huawei and Nvidia (NVDA). Right now, SMEE (Shanghai Micro Electronics Equipment) has no EUV capabilities and is not in the position to compete directly with ASML top tier equipment. Moreover, investors may understand this segment as a high-entrance cost market, where massive investment is needed to develop new products, subjected to a variable success rate. As this is a future risk, ASML has strong fundamentals to remain tech dominant with strategic tailwinds in the medium term, at least.

Fundamental Score

In my research, I compare several target firms that are selected based on an appropriate investment thesis. Right now, some of the relevant theses include Artificial Intelligence Hardware and Applications, Infrastructure and Cybersecurity, and Defense among others. Firms are compared in terms of four elements. Initially, the Cash Discipline is evaluated in terms of how well the operation converts cash. The second is related to the total yield an investor receives considering the payout and the potential reinvestment of cash at current prices. An analysis of growth components is the next category, in order to understand commercial success and how it translates into real profitability growth. The last one is a measure of momentum, as it is a strong performance indicator and also may impose an important demand engine due to the prevalence of passive investments.

In general, ASML is rated as a Hold, with strong performance on cash and momentum grades. This indicates that, in the current price, there is a mix of strong fundamentals but also the momentum trade related to AI in general. On Figure 1, an evaluation of last 4 years of cash flow management is performed. The numbers are scaled by last year sales to allow for comparison with other firms. ASML generates consistent operating cash flow, is such levels that is sufficient to absorb required investments and return capital to shareholders. In general, an increase in the debt level, as occurred in Q2/2023, is directly related to the cash variation.

The positive management of cash, reflected in the Cash Grade, can also be perceived in the balance sheet indicators. Since the acquisition of Hermes Microvision in 2016, ASML is not actively engaged in large M&A transactions. The goodwill recorded remained relatively flat, which implied in steady reduction of the ratio to other long-term assets. In this way, one can understand the firm’s numbers as organically generated. The debt level is also being reduced since 2022, as a result of an increase in shareholders equity from Retained Earnings (i.e. from operating results). In terms of liquidity, current ratio reduced to 1.3 in the period. It is hard to make a shorter-term assessment due to report limitations on the Current Liabilities, but a long-term analysis depicts an increase in unearned revenue, linked to production slot prepayments for EUV equipment since 2017, but more relevantly after 2022. Figure 2 illustrates the balance sheet indicators.

As for the other positive grade, ASML has a strong momentum situation. The firm is pushing towards the higher band of the one-year price range and above its 200 days moving average. In terms of valuation, its PE is aligned with my market average calculation, which considers a market-cap weighted average of forward PE. At last, the firm has a positive news sentiment in an AI classification method using recent news published about the company. These indicators are available on Figure 3. On Seeking Alpha, ASML momentum grade is B, with a strong price performance in comparison to all sector medians.

As for the Yield Grade, ASML is at the median of the researched companies. In Figure 4, ASML (the red dot) is compared to the other target companies and position itself with a 2.6% FCF Yield and a controlled debt environment. Initially, there is an indication of pricing power on the margins recorded. A comparison of ASML and Cadence (CDNS), which operate with completely different offers in the same semiconductor supply landscape, ASML depicts a premium Net Income. Moreover, the consistent performance is the result of a stable margin environment, as shown in Figure 5 and an improvement in the investment performance compared to the gross margin, as depicted in Figure 6. In this measure, which both Capex and R&D expenses are taken into consideration and compared to the Gross Margin generated, it is possible to evaluate the capacity of the firm to fund development efforts from its operation, without the need to further dilute capital or generate debt. In the last two years, it improved from 43% to 36% in this last quarter.

On a more negative note, current growth grade lags the median of researched firms. This is not an effect of ASML being stagnant but rather a relative measure, indicating that when compared to other target firms, mostly in the AI segment, there is a gap on the numbers of the firm, mostly when this comparison is performed against GPUs, AI applications, and infra construction. Figure 7 characterizes the revenues and gross margins of the firm in the last 20 quarters. Considering a flat gross margin scenario, ASML records revenue increase, but also a volatility resulting from natural cycles in the demand of foundries. In 2022, there was a supply chain disruption in the industry, that followed a demand exuberance that ended in a reduced customer appetite in 2024. This year’s story seems to be linked to export controls and a weak demand for part of the semiconductor DUV industry. In a comparison with Cadence, one can perceive that this behavior is not an idiosyncratic issue of ASML but rather a general market situation. In such case, investors may understand that an equity position in ASML will provide a different feature and risk factor exposure than other AI related stocks, such as Nvidia, for example. In the long run the expected growth in the industry should prevail and benefit ASML as well.

Conclusion

ASML published solid Q3 numbers, aligned with a growing market trend on the AI supply chain. The company’s strategic position is unique, as it enjoys a virtual monopoly in a critical component for the production of high-capacity chipsets. This situation embeds a consistent growth prospect, potentially volatile but long-term consistent, and the maintenance of pricing power. The firm’s cash generation and management is rational and cover both the investment and R&D needs and the shareholders current remuneration. Forward cash flow growth measures, arising from the future continuation of current results, may trigger strong price performance.

On the risk side, in the fundamental scoring system, a higher grade on momentum, compared to Yield and Growth may indicate that future results are priced closer to perfection than they could be, so investors keeping ASML in equity portfolios should evaluate the position under the scrutiny of market developments and potential short-term volatility in the stock is likely.

As a combination of both findings, ASML is a Hold and may be part of a suitability evaluation for a long position in equity portfolios, which will depend on each investor's personal situation.

data.title: Netflix Earnings Preview: Q3 2025
data.description:
Netflix Inc. (NFLX) will report third quarter 2025 results on Tuesday, Oct. 21, 2025. Here are the key numbers that we're watching.

The preview

Expectations have remained stable throughout the third quarter. In the previous three-month period, the company provided guidance for a stable outlook, in line with consensus. Visible Alpha consensus expects $11.5 billion in revenue for the third quarter and $45.1 billion for the full year 2025. Revenue is expected to be supported by continued new membership and monetization. Offering a range of pricing and plans, combined with continuing growth in the ads business, is expected to further increase monetization. Consensus expects the operating margin to increase to 31.5% in the third quarter and to 30.3% for full year 2025.

The company expects to grow revenue by increasing engagement trends and reducing churn while offering more diverse entertainment products. Gaming and the growth of ads could be key drivers in 2025. According to consensus, analysts now expect the company to generate a 30.3% margin, up from 29.6% in previous estimates, on expected revenue of $45.1 billion and $13.6 billion in operating profit in full year 2025.

In the spring, management highlighted that the Ad Tier enables lower prices. The company stated in the earnings call that they expect ad revenue to roughly double year over year again in 2025. Since early 2025, the ad-supported revenue estimate has been moving higher into the third quarter earnings release. While expectations are still below the initial forecast of $1.6 billion back in early 2024, the trend is improving and is now at $1.1 billion.

Netflix remains upbeat about the long-term opportunity, given the size of its user base. Co-CEO Gregory Peters explained that 2025 will be the year that the ads business will step up to the plate to swing. Currently, consensus projects total ad-supported revenue for full year 2027 to expand to $6.5 billion, but down from the expected $7.4 billion in 2024. There is a significant range of views on the magnitude of growth. For full year 2027, the analyst estimates range has widened from $3.5 billion to $9.2 billion to between $3.8 billion and $13.8 billion.

Based on Visible Alpha consensus, the operating profit margin is expected to grow from 26.7% in full year 2024 to 35.1% in full year 2027, an increase of 70 basis points since last quarter. Currently, consensus estimates the operating margin to exceed 32% by the end of 2026. There is significant debate among analysts with respect to full-year 2027 margin estimates, which range from 32% to 37%. This margin growth is expected to take full-year 2024 expected diluted EPS from $20.22 to $39.77, or 30x the full-year 2027 P/E, significantly higher than the 25x it traded in spring. The current consensus target price has ticked up to $1,400, or nearly 17% upside from the current levels.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: Ethan Allen Interiors: Target Price Is Justified By Strategic Operations And Valuation
data.description:
It’s been three months since my first analysis of Ethan Allen Interiors, Inc. (NYSE:ETD). And after a 6.2% stock price decrease, my hold rating was justified. I also think it helped that my projected revenue of $614M was close to the reported amount of $614.6M at the end of the year. Right now, ETD still faces several challenges. Yet, its solid Balance Sheet and new market opportunities may help boost or at least stabilize its performance in FY 2026. Valuation still shows underpricing, but the question about whether or not an upside is possible should be considered again. Technicals still show bullish signals, just what I expected before, but downside risks are there.

Challenges

Inflation remains a primary challenge for businesses within the consumer discretionary sector. Ethan Allen Interiors, Inc. remains vulnerable to risks as inflation continues to affect pricing flexibility, demand, and cost pressures. That is why its Q4 2025 performance turned out to be in line with my revenue expectations. Its operating revenue amounted to $160.4M, down by -4.9% YoY from $168.6M. This brought sales to a total of $614.6M. Reduced production volume and units delivered remained the primary challenge it faced during the quarter. Even now, I believe ETD still experiences this to manage the impact of inflation. Note that the Fed estimates that inflation climbed to over 3.0% last month. This would be the first time that inflation would reach this level after seven months. The trend may continue as the Fed expects that the target of 2.0% won’t be met until 2028. With that, discretionary spending may still be soft in H1 2026.

Another challenge is the weak housing market. While the median home sales have already gone down to $410,800, pre-pandemic levels are still much lower. Although it is 2% lower than in my previous coverage, the price is still above $400,000. This continues to soften housing demand, which, in turn, also softens the demand for new home furnishings and decorations.

Considering all this, I’m keeping my 2026 estimates the same as in my previous coverage. Another revenue drop of -5% makes sense for me. From the most recent revenue, it may decrease to $584M this FY. And since I set COGS and revenue as a percentage of revenue, the operating profit will decrease in the same way. To have a broader view, I also estimated depreciation and capex using the straight-line method to compute NOPAT and EBITDA. The consolation is that I expect ETD to remain profitable as it stays prudent with its cost and inventory management.

Drivers/Mitigants

Despite the challenges it faces today, ETD may benefit from some favorable market changes.

1.Interest Rate Cuts And Decreasing Mortgage Rates Can Bring Some Positive Spillovers. Last month, the Fed decided to cut the interest rate by 25 bps to 4.25% after keeping it untouched for 10 months. This move aims to boost labor market conditions. And if this succeeds, higher employment and wages may also take place. I think this can help partially offset the impact of inflation and increase confidence among consumers and borrowers. As a result, discretionary spending may increase. And I think this is timely for home repairs or renovations ahead of the holiday season and winter.

On top of that, interest rate cuts can lower adjustable-rate mortgages or ARMs and HELOCs. It can also lower 30-year mortgage rates since these are directly tied to the 10-year treasury yields. The yield typically drops with the interest rate. As you can see, the 30-year mortgage rate is approaching 6% again after staying at/near 7% for quite some time. These changes could possibly entice homeowners and raise the demand for ETD’s products.

2.Strategic Location Makes ETD More Competitive. Many companies in the home furnishings industry are vulnerable to new tariffs due to their inflationary nature. This is especially risky for those heavily reliant on product and raw material imports. That may not be the case for ETD due to its strong domestic production footprint in the US and Honduras. As I explained in my previous article, Honduras has more tariff insulation. As you can see, most countries face new tariffs of at least 15%. The remaining ones, including Honduras, pay 10%. This means that ETD could possibly manage cost pressures than its competitors who source products from countries with higher tariffs.

Moreover, Honduras is among those countries with low labor costs or monthly wages. I look up various sources on the Internet. The Global Living Wage Org estimated that the average monthly wage in Honduras was only $277. But since several years have already passed since its publication, I looked for other sources. According to Playroll, the average monthly wage in 2025 is $620, but this may vary. For microbusinesses, the average is only about $370. In small and medium businesses, wages are about $370 and $550, respectively. And for large companies, this can reach $860. Either way, wages in Honduras are cheap, so ETD enjoys lower labor expenses.

As we combine the impact of lower tariffs and labor expenses in Honduras, ETD may be more competitive in pricing and marketing strategies without sacrificing sales and profits. And I think this still works to its advantage despite lower sales last quarter. In fact, written orders for the retail segment increased by 1.6%. In addition, its operating leverage decreased from 45.1% to 44.3%, which means that ETD is less cost-sensitive as of this writing. While this was not enough to offset the sales decrease, it helped ETD maintain positive earnings.

3.Strong Liquidity Means Business Sustainability And Dividend Longevity. ETD remains efficient to ensure positive profits. One important aspect is its prudent inventory management. The decreasing inventory level in its Balance Sheet is consistent with its demand. That way it can optimize its production and manage costs and expenses. It still works to its advantage as it remains profitable with positive cash inflows.

As we can see, its operating cash flow has stayed adequate to cover capex and dividends in the past 11 quarters. So, it’s not surprising that its cash level also remains stable above $100M. It doesn’t have debts, which means that ETD generates enough earnings and/or cash inflows to sustain its operations and dividend payouts on its own. Hence, it has enough capital buffer to ensure its liquidity.

Valuation

As I revisit the stock, I notice its price appears to be most reactive with book value per share, not EPS nor sales per share. With that, I decided to use P/B to assess it. As you can see, the stock trades at 1.48x its book value versus the five-year average of 1.50x. If we use it to derive the fair value of ETD, the target price, or TP, will be $28.45. This shows that ETD trades at a reasonable price. It is neither cheap nor expensive relative to its FY 2021-2025 average.

To have a more forward-looking approach, I also used the Dividend Discount Model. Note that I based its annualized dividends of $1.56 on the quarterly regular payout of $0.39. Its dividend growth rate is the average growth rate since 2021 to exclude the impact of the pandemic. Meanwhile, I estimated its cost of equity using the capital asset pricing model. Note that I set its risk premium at 6.0%, which is slightly above the standard rate of 5.0% to price in more risks. Lastly, I estimated its Beta using the SLOPE function on the daily price change of the stock versus the S&P 500 on MS Excel. Using all these numbers, I derived a TP of $31.39. If I add a 5% risk consideration, the TP will be $29.81. This shows some upside on the stock price.

Technicals

ETD’s trend has weakened in the past two weeks following its consolidation for three months. The price has already crossed below the 50-day and 200-day SMAs, showing that the previous uptrend after the correction may not hold. Right now, the price is still consolidating despite its slight dip. The 50-day SMA is still flattish above the 200-day SMA. This shows that market sentiments are still neutral and that there is still a chance that the price will increase again.

Its MACD and RSI explain the logic behind the recent trend. The MACD line is still below the signal, which shows that the selling volume is not over yet. It is also within negative territory along with the histogram, which means that selling volume still outpaces buying volume. But it is worth noting that the histogram slightly improved in the past three trading days. And as you can see, the stock had net buying for two consecutive days. This can tell us that buyers found new support at $27. RSI also shows recent overselling that prompted reentries. At this point, it is already within normal range. The buying strength index may not be as strong as it was two days ago. But it is still closer to the oversold region, so there's still some room to push the price up again.

Conclusion

Ethan Allen Interiors, Inc. experiences macroeconomic challenges affecting discretionary spending and demand for its products. Yet, its strategic manufacturing footprint and robust liquidity could possibly support its profitability, dividend longevity, and operational sustainability. This also positions ETD well for possible spillovers from the recent rate cut and decreasing mortgage rates. That is why I think its current valuation is justifiable with some upside. I still advise caution, but I believe that an optimistic outlook makes sense. Technicals are still neutral but show some buying opportunities after the recent overselling. I’m upgrading my rating on this stock from hold to buy.

data.title: Tuya: Quiet Execution, Strong Margins, And A Billion-Dollar Cash Cushion
data.description:
Tuya Inc. (NYSE:TUYA) is performing as well as I expected when I covered it in May, after Q1 2025 results came out, albeit the stock has been mostly flat. The company is staying profitable while growing, and I’m surprised at how little the market seems to care.

To be fair, it’s a Chinese tech company, and given the current macroeconomic and geopolitical environment, it does track that investors aren’t exactly flocking to buy it up. At the same time, I don’t think it’s unreasonable to start buying a company that is quietly turning into a global platform business. The customer base is huge (there are 1.5 million users on its AI Developer platform), and its AI-enabled tools are doing well in the market.

Those feed into the numbers, and revenue, margins, and cash flow are all up. As we know, if it gets big and important enough, deals can always be made to cushion the effect of tariffs and associated risks on the business.

Right now, there’s a valuation gap between how Tuya is performing and its price, and that’s why I’m staying long.

The Story Holds Up Operationally and Financially

I’ll start this section with a recap of Tuya’s Q2 2025 results. First things first: the business model is working very well. Revenue was up 9% YoY to $80.1 million, and the business’s 3 segments all trended up. We have PaaS growing 7%, SaaS and others up 16%, and Smart Solutions rising by 17%. Clearly, there’s a nice balance between all three’s contributions to the bottom line.

With gross margins, SaaS was the biggest contributor at 72%. PaaS didn’t do badly either (48.7%), and Smart Solutions came in at 22.5%. Those are nice numbers, bringing up the total gross margin to 48.4%, 40 basis points higher than the same period in 2024. Remember that all of this happened against a backdrop of tariffs and supply chain friction, so the consistency tells me that Tuya is getting its pricing and product mix right. Oh, and net profit was 3x what it was in the same period last year. The company also has a very flexible balance sheet (~$1 billion in cash helps with that), and debt is nonexistent. So, there’s a lot of opportunity to be had by reinvesting in the business.

However, the main reason I’m so confident in Tuya’s prospects is its evolution. It’s now growing beyond being an IoT platform. As I mentioned earlier, the company has over 1.5 million developers on its platform, and they’re building on top of advanced AI capabilities. During the Q2 2025 earnings call, for example, CFO Alex Yang said that “as of June 13, 2025, 93% of Tuya's shipped product categories were equipped with AI capabilities.” Along with that, the company’s AI agents now handle over 150 million interactions every day. That represents incredibly important commercial proof that Tuya is becoming a backbone for AI-driven products.

If you also look at things from an execution perspective, there’s a lot to like. The company’s Europe business has seen double-digit growth in categories like home appliances, telecoms, and real estate, which are the main drivers in APAC, and North America is seeing a lot of traction in AI pet and entertainment products. In China, e-commerce growth is the way forward. Overall, I think the business mix is a big part of why Tuya hasn’t been too bothered by tariff and currency risks, among others.

Now, none of these would add much to the story if we didn’t see the effect in the numbers. Management has managed to keep non-GAAP operating expenses lower than $40 million for the last few quarters, and they’re spending just enough to keep the AI and developer engines running while profitability is stable. I don’t think it’s common to see companies at this stage in their growth cycle manage the balance so well.

My Near-Term Outlook, And The Risks

Tuya has done pretty well across H1 2025, and I believe that the impact of the next stage of the company’s growth trajectory on the narrative largely depends on H2’s results. We already know that management still expects issues around trade friction and tariff uncertainty, and the US government’s recent moves don’t exactly inspire confidence. But we should still see steady growth and margin stability, so what I’m watching for is how the company executes through all that noise.

I expect revenue around $82-84 million for Q3 (+5-7% QoQ), and non-GAAP operating margins around the 11% mark, which is flat. The same goes for net margins and positive operating cash flow. This is in spite of the tariffs and supply chain issues, and my bullishness comes from the relative stability of the last three months. Of course, the US tariff cycle hit discretionary electronics earlier this year, and Tuya has indirect exposure. Say a manufacturer shifts production from China to Vietnam or Mexico. Tuya has to follow its customers in cases like these, and it adds short-term cost friction. Many of these countries are still talking and trying to settle tariff issues, but who absorbs the added expenses is unclear, and that can affect order timing. Again, I don’t think it will affect the company’s sales much, but I’ll keep an eye on it.

When it comes to developer engagement and AI monetization, we know that the company already has the people in its ecosystem to drive added growth and value add. What I’m looking for is more AI monetization as the next step, and that should flow through in the SaaS and PaaS numbers. I think new pricing tiers tied to AI functionality can be a real winner here, albeit Tuya needs to handle it carefully. If it can achieve higher conversion rates (developer activity to paid usage and recurring revenue), then we will begin to see the operating leverage start to really compound.

That leads me on to the company’s dollar-based net expansion rate (DBNER). This is a metric that tells us how well Tuya is managing to expand its existing customers’ use of its platform and generate more revenue from them. In Q2, that number fell to 114% from 127% in the previous period last year, mostly because of tariff-related headwinds, and I expect it to start trending up over the next few quarters slowly. That way, we can see that customers’ spending on the platform is normalizing.

Now, there are other risks, like increased competition in the space. AWS and Alibaba Cloud are huge competitors, even if they’re not as specialized as Tuya. Still, they have the advantage of size and reputation, so they might want to compete with price undercuts or better OEM relationships. It’s up to Tuya to use its developer network and platform stickiness to keep delivering what customers want, really, and that’s the one way to keep its advantage.

I’m treating Q3 and Q4 as a durability test for the company, because it doesn’t need to post breakout growth to justify a Buy for me. What we need to see is consistency, and if that happens, then there’s a lot of upside in the current valuation. We’ll talk about that next.

The Current Price Is Relatively Cheap

I don’t believe that Tuya’s valuation today matches the quality of its results. The company ended Q2 with ~$1.01 billion in net cash and investments and no meaningful debt. That translates to around $1.65 per ADS or ordinary share in cash, using a fully diluted share count of 610 million. That is a very strong balance sheet for a company of this size, especially because it’s already profitable and is a major cash generator.

If we annualize H1’s revenue (Q1+Q2) of around $155 million, we get a run-rate of $310 million. With that figure, I think it’s easy to see why the market is mispricing Tuya. Right now, its EV/Sales multiple is around 1.9x, its enterprise value is $620 million, and the market cap is $1.44 billion.

Let’s move the EV/Sales multiple up to 2.5x, and the company’s true value is much closer to $1.8 billion. If we move it up to 3x, it’s near $2 billion. Keep in mind that both assumed multiples are much lower than the sector median of 3.6x, so that’s a lot of upside currently sitting on the table.

I don’t think those are aggressive assumptions. Tuya is profitable, it has a ~48% gross margin, ~25% non-GAAP net margin, and positive operating cash flow. The business model is executing nicely, and operating expenses have been within a steady dollar range for several quarters while revenue is growing. That is consistency that gives the company the kind of financial leverage that most mid-stage platform companies don’t reach this quickly.

My Recommendation

In my view, the market discounting the stock because of tariff uncertainty and its Chinese roots is a good opportunity to get in at a good price before the dust settles. This is a business that is incredibly liquid, profitable, and paying dividends while growing steadily. At the current price, you’re paying roughly two times forward sales for a company with one of the cleanest balance sheets in the Chinese tech space and an expanding global ecosystem, and that’s a trade I’ll take anytime.

I’m confident that the business will continue its cost discipline, building recurring PaaS and SaaS revenue, and growing margins. If that happens, I see a fair bit of material upside over the next year because then, the market should have started looking at this as a sustainable buy.

data.title: VCE Over VDY: A Deep Dive Into Canada's Top Vanguard ETFs
data.description:
I believe that the Canadian Equity market represents a very interesting investment opportunity, and in this analysis, I will look at two of Vanguard ETFs aimed at this market: the Vanguard FTSE Canadian High Dividend Yield Index ETF (TSX:VDY:CA) and the Vanguard FTSE Canada Index ETF (TSX:VCE:CA). Both of them are very strong ETFs that provide investors with simple ways to get exposure to this market, with very different strategies. They are both buys, but I will give VCE a slightly better outlook against VDY. The reason behind that is that both ETFs are showing similar returns and volatility metrics, but VCE has the benefits of being more affordable, better diversified, and more representative of the broad Canadian economy. For investors that are prioritizing income above everything, VDY is an interesting option, but I believe that its high concentration on just two sectors is too much of a risk over the long term and I don’t think that the dividend premium is enough to make the fund equally as good as VCE.

The Core Strategic Divide

The difference between the two ETFs is coming from their two very different strategies. VDY is tracking the FTSE Canada High Dividend Yield Index. To build its portfolio, the index is starting from all Canadian equities in the FTSE GEIS classification and ranks companies by dividend yield. The bottom 50% is excluded and the rest of the companies are weighted by market-cap. In short, this index is composed of the highest yielding equities of the FTSE Canada index. On the other hand, VCE has a more traditional approach to equity markets. The fund is tracking the FTSE Canada Index, simply replicating the Canadian equity market without additional filters and a market-cap weighting methodology. The difference of strategy results in very different portfolios and risks, despite very close returns.

With its focus on high yield equities, the portfolio of VDY is mechanically more concentrated. The fund holds 57 companies, mainly financials (55.78%) and energy (26.64%) companies, making for more than 80% of the portfolio. If you want to invest in Canada and go with VDY, you will almost only invest in these two sectors, and the result will be a cyclical portfolio, not very representative of the broad Canadian economy. In addition, the individual holdings are also very concentrated: the top 10 account for 67.74% of the portfolio, and the top 3 alone account for 33.14% of the portfolio with two large-cap banking companies: the Royal Bank of Canada (RY:CA) at 15.17% and The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD:CA) at 9.98%. This is of course completed by an energy company: Enbridge Inc. (ENB:CA) at 7.99%.

VCE’s profile is very different. The fund holds more companies: 79. It also has a higher degree of diversification both in terms of sectoral splits and individual holdings. Representing the Canadian economy, the financial industry still represents a significant 36.36% of the portfolio, but this is a lot less than VDY. In addition, the fund is invested in other sectors that you will not really find with VDY, as they don’t provide investors with significant income streams: Basic Materials (14.38%) and Technology (11.88%). A significant part of the tech exposure is driven by the second largest holding of the fund: Shopify Inc. (SHOP:CA), with 6.66% of the portfolio. The surge of e-commerce is a major global trend that VDY fails to capture due to its concentration on Financials and Energy, and for a broad ETF tracking a country’s performance, this is a clear miss. The individual concentration of the fund is also much lower (while still relatively high due to the market-cap weighting methodology). The top 10 holdings account for 42.16%, and the top 3 for 19.52%. I believe that the reach of VCE is clearly a strength against VDY, as the fund appears to be more robust (less cyclical), while also capturing innovative growth companies.
